# nomad_plugin (Data Source)

Lookup a plugin by ID. The aim of this datasource is to determine whether a particular plugin exists on the cluster, to find information on the health and availability of the plugin, and to optionally wait for the plugin before performing actions the require an available plugin controller.

If a plugin with the specified ID does not exist and the datasource is not configured to wait, it will result in an error. For simple existence checks, use the `nomad_plugins` listing datasource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "nomad_plugin" "ebs" {
  plugin_id        = "aws-ebs0"
  wait_for_healthy = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `plugin_id` (String) Plugin ID

### Optional

- `wait_for_healthy` (Boolean) Wait for to be backed by a specified number of controllers
- `wait_for_registration` (Boolean) Wait for the plugin to be registered in Noamd

### Read-Only

- `controller_required` (Boolean)
- `controllers_expected` (Number)
- `controllers_healthy` (Number)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `nodes` (List of Object) Available nodes for this plugin (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--nodes))
- `nodes_expected` (Number)
- `nodes_healthy` (Number)
- `plugin_provider` (String)
- `plugin_provider_version` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--nodes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `nodes`

Read-Only:

- `healthy` (Boolean)
- `healthy_description` (String)
- `name` (String)
